summary: The final Hallelujah Psalms (134, 146–150) form a grand crescendo of praise, calling all creation—from night-watch priests to sun, moon, stars, and every living creature—to worship the LORD who creates, sustains, redeems, and reigns forever.
psalm_rows_html:
PsalmTypeAuthorSituationKey ThemeNotable VerseNT Connection
134Song of AscentsUnknownNight watchmen serving in the templeBlessing flows from Zion; servants praising God through the nightv.2 — "Lift up your hands in the sanctuary and praise the LORD."Heb 13:15 — the continual sacrifice of praise through Christ
146Hallelujah PsalmUnknownGeneral call to trust God rather than human rulersOnly God is a reliable helper; he cares for the vulnerable and reigns foreverv.3 — "Do not put your trust in princes, in human beings, who cannot save."Luke 4:18–19 — Jesus' mission mirrors the LORD's deeds in vv.7–9
147Hallelujah PsalmUnknown (post-exilic)Jerusalem restored after exileGod's sovereign power over creation and history, paired with tender care for the humblev.3 — "He heals the brokenhearted and binds up their wounds."John 1:1–14 — God's word that "runs swiftly" (v.15) becomes flesh in Jesus Christ
148Cosmic HallelujahUnknownUniversal summons to praise from all creationAll creation—heavenly and earthly—is called to praise its Creatorv.13 — "His name alone is exalted; his splendor is above the earth and the heavens."Rev 5:13 — every creature in heaven and earth giving praise to God and the Lamb
149Victory HymnUnknownAnticipation or celebration of God's triumph through his peopleGod's faithful are honored as instruments of his righteous judgmentv.4 — "The LORD takes delight in his people; he crowns the humble with victory."Rev 19:1–6 — the great "Hallelujah" celebrating God's final victory
150Grand DoxologyUnknownClosing doxology of the entire PsalterTotal, universal, full-voiced praise to God from all living beings with every instrumentv.6 — "Let everything that has breath praise the LORD."Phil 2:10–11 — every knee bowing and every tongue confessing Christ's lordship
